title: Classification of topological quantum matter with symmetries

section_map.id: Introduction / Opening motivation and topology-symmetry contrast
section_map.title: Introduction / Opening motivation and topology-symmetry contrast
section_map.summary: The review introduces topological insulators as symmetry-protected topological phases: short-range-entangled gapped phases whose topological properties rely on symmetry, since without time-reversal symmetry they can be adiabatically deformed to trivial insulators without closing the bulk gap; source anchor: Introduction / Opening motivation and topology-symmetry contrast (lines 220-229).

section_map.title: Symmetries / Spatial-vs-nonspatial split
section_map.summary: The paper distinguishes nonspatial symmetries as symmetries acting locally in position space and formalizes the split by calling a symmetry spatial when it acts on spatial indices and nonspatial when it does not; on-site symmetries are a special nonspatial case; source anchor: Introduction / Overview of topological materials and Symmetries / Spatial-vs-nonspatial split (lines 259-263, 534-545).

section_map.title: Symmetries / Time-reversal symmetry
section_map.summary: For a noninteracting first-quantized Hamiltonian, time-reversal symmetry imposes U_T^dagger H^* U_T = H, and the generic symmetry-class analysis yields U_T^* U_T = +/- 1, determining whether T^2 acts as identity or fermion parity; source anchor: Symmetries / Time-reversal symmetry (lines 553-669; Eq. DEFTimeReversal).

section_map.title: Symmetries / Particle-hole symmetry
section_map.summary: For a noninteracting first-quantized Hamiltonian, particle-hole symmetry imposes U_C^dagger H^T U_C = -H, equivalently -U_C^dagger H^* U_C = H, and the generic symmetry-class analysis yields U_C^* U_C = +/- 1; source anchor: Symmetries / Particle-hole symmetry (lines 675-724; Eq. DEFChargeConjugation).

section_map.title: Symmetries / Chiral symmetry
section_map.summary: Chiral symmetry, obtained as the product of time-reversal and particle-hole symmetry, constrains a first-quantized Hamiltonian by U_S^dagger H U_S = -H and, after phase choice, {H,U_S} = 0 with U_S^2 = 1, yielding a block-off-diagonal form in the chiral basis; source anchor: Symmetries / Chiral symmetry (lines 775-850; Eq. chiral_Def_eq).

section_map.title: Introduction / Opening motivation and topology-symmetry contrast
section_map.summary: Topological quantum matter is framed as phases distinguished by topologically nontrivial quantum-mechanical wave functions rather than by spontaneous symmetry breaking or local order parameters; source anchor: Introduction / Opening motivation and topology-symmetry contrast and Introduction / Scope and organization of the review (lines 187-230, 331-335).

section_map.title: Bloch-BdG Eigenfunction Notation for Topological Invariants
section_map.summary: The Topological invariants subsection introduces the invariant discussion in terms of Bloch-BdG eigenfunctions |u^a(k,r)> and energies epsilon^a(k,r) defined by H(k,r)|u^a(k,r)> = epsilon^a(k,r)|u^a(k,r)>; source anchor: Topological invariants / Bloch-BdG eigenfunction setup (lines 2041-2068).

section_map.id: Fully gapped free fermion systems and topological defects / Topological defects
section_map.title: Real AZ Classification by s minus Delta
section_map.summary: For real Altland-Zirnbauer symmetry classes, the classification of topological defects depends only on the single congruence class s - delta = s - d + D modulo 8; source anchor: Fully gapped free fermion systems and topological defects / Topological defects (lines 1997-2013).
